title: Set up Playwright E2E test infrastructure for the deployed client
body:
## Issue description

Nova-Circle has solid Vitest/jsdom unit and component tests for domain logic, policies, and individual UI components, but has **no end-to-end browser automation**. Critical user journeys — authentication redirects, group/event navigation, event creation, and RSVP — are tested only at the unit level (with mocked MSAL and API clients). A standalone Playwright E2E suite running against the real deployed (or locally served) application is needed to close this gap.

This issue covers the foundational infrastructure work required before any individual E2E scenario can be written or run in CI.

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

### Playwright installation and configuration
- Add `@playwright/test` as a dev dependency inside `client/`.
- Create `client/playwright.config.ts` targeting the deployed (or locally served) base URL, configurable via a `PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL` environment variable (default: `http://localhost:5173` for local dev).
- Configure at minimum the `chromium` browser; add `firefox` and `webkit` as optional extras.
- Set a reasonable global timeout (e.g. 30 s per test, 10 s per assertion).
- Enable Playwright's HTML reporter plus JUnit XML output for CI artifact upload.
- Place all E2E specs under `client/e2e/` and wire the `testDir` accordingly.

### MSAL authentication strategy for E2E
- Real MSAL interactive flows are not suitable for CI automation.
- Implement a **storage-state authentication helper** that:
  1. Reads a pre-authenticated Playwright storage state JSON from an environment-injected path (`PLAYWRIGHT_AUTH_STATE_FILE`), **or**
  2. Falls back to a headless MSAL username/password sign-in using dedicated Azure AD test-user credentials (`PLAYWRIGHT_TEST_USER_EMAIL`, `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST_USER_PASSWORD`) that are stored as GitHub Actions secrets.
- Provide a global Playwright setup file (`client/e2e/global-setup.ts`) that produces and saves the storage state before the main test run begins.
- Unauthenticated tests (e.g. redirect tests) must explicitly use a fresh browser context with no stored auth state.

### Test helper utilities
- Create `client/e2e/helpers/auth.ts` — exports `authenticatedContext()` and `unauthenticatedContext()` helpers.
- Create `client/e2e/helpers/api.ts` — thin wrapper for seeding/tearing down test data via the backend API using **normal authenticated requests** with the test user's bearer token (reused from the Playwright/MSAL storage state) so tests can set up known state deterministically. For purely local runs against a `NODE_ENV=test` backend, this helper may optionally support the `X-Test-*` header bypass, but E2E runs against the real deployed site must not rely on that bypass.
- All helpers must be strongly typed.

### npm scripts
- Add `"test:e2e": "playwright test"` to `client/package.json`.
- Add `"test:e2e:ui": "playwright test --ui"` for local interactive runs.
- Add `"test:e2e:report": "playwright show-report"` for reviewing HTML reports.

### GitHub Actions CI job
- Add a new `e2e` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that:
  - Runs after the existing `client` job succeeds.
  - Installs Playwright browsers with `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium`.
  - Requires `PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL`, `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST_USER_EMAIL`, `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST_USER_PASSWORD` (and any Azure AD tenant/client IDs) from GitHub Actions secrets/vars.
  - Runs `npm run test:e2e` inside `client/`.
  - Uploads the Playwright HTML report and JUnit XML as workflow artifacts.
  - On failure, uploads screenshots and traces.
- The job must not block merges on `main` if the deployment target is unavailable; use `continue-on-error: false` but gate the job on a `RUN_E2E` environment variable or the presence of the required secrets (use `if: vars.RUN_E2E == 'true'` or equivalent).

### Page Object Model (POM)
- Establish a `client/e2e/pages/` directory with base Page Object classes for reuse across scenarios:
  - `LoginPage` — locators for sign-in button, brand header.
  - `GroupsListPage` — locators for group cards, new-group form.
  - `GroupDetailPage` — locators for event list, group name.
  - `EventDetailPage` — locators for title, RSVP buttons, attendee list.
  - `EventCreatePage` — locators for form inputs, capture textarea, submit.
- POMs must not contain test assertions; they provide locator accessors and action methods only.

## Additional notes

- The backend exposes `X-Test-User-Id`, `X-Test-Display-Name` headers as an auth bypass **only in `NODE_ENV=test`** builds (`src/shared/auth/auth-middleware.ts`). These headers are intended for backend and integration tests; the Playwright E2E suite running against the real deployed site (configured with `NODE_ENV=production`) must seed data through the real API using test-user authentication, not via this bypass.
- `client/src/auth/msal-config.ts` reads `VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID`; the deployed build already has these baked in.
- Client routes are defined in `client/src/router.tsx`. Authenticated routes are wrapped by `<ProtectedRoute>` which redirects to `/login` when unauthenticated.
- Do not add Playwright as a dependency of the root `package.json`; keep it scoped to `client/`.
- The `.gitignore` should be updated to exclude `client/playwright-report/`, `client/test-results/`, and `client/e2e/.auth/`.
- Follow the project's privacy-first principle: never log or store test user passwords in artifacts or logs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `@playwright/test` is listed as a dev dependency in `client/package.json`.
- [ ] `client/playwright.config.ts` exists, targets `PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL`, and is valid (passes `npx playwright test --list` without errors).
- [ ] `client/e2e/` directory exists with at least a placeholder spec and the `global-setup.ts` and `helpers/` structure.
- [ ] Page Object classes exist for Login, GroupsList, GroupDetail, EventDetail, and EventCreate.
- [ ] `npm run test:e2e` in `client/` runs Playwright and exits 0 when the target is reachable and auth state is valid.
- [ ] A new `e2e` job exists in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that installs browsers, runs `npm run test:e2e`, and uploads HTML report and JUnit XML artifacts.
- [ ] The CI job is conditional on `vars.RUN_E2E == 'true'` (or secrets presence) so it does not fail PRs when no deployment target is available.
- [ ] `.gitignore` excludes `playwright-report/`, `test-results/`, and `e2e/.auth/` inside `client/`.
- [ ] No test user credentials are committed to the repository or appear in CI logs.
